title: dichrocephala hamiltonii
intro: Dichrocephala hamiltonii, also known as the Chinese ironwort or yellow buphthalmum, is a flowering plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Dichrocephala hamiltonii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic potential.
